name: Joe Sweeney
subject: Goal TONIGHT - Our 2020 Vision
body:
Derek, **Republicans are getting fired up.** From Hillsborough to Coös, Rockingham to Grafton, and Carroll to Cheshire, conservatives in the Granite State are showing up to packed county, city, and town committee meetings. We're definitely seeing Republicans wake up, fully knowing how important 2019 & 2020 will be for our State and our party. All of us need a State Republican Party that has the resources to compete with the Democrats on messaging, field organizing, and GOTV operations. They rely on mountains of out-of-state donations - the NHGOP relies on members like you to support our organization. There's going to be a lot of noise with all the Democrats coming to New Hampshire looking to take on President Trump. While the Democrats are distracted, we'll be focused on making gains in city and local elections this year and recruiting candidates for the State House, Senate, and Executive Council. 2020 can be a great year for the New Hampshire Republican Party - we can take back the House, Senate, and Executive Council while keeping the Corner Office and sending new representation to D.C. from New Hampshire. We can do all of this if you invest in our party by [renewing or activating your membership before tonight's midnight goal.](http://email.robly.com/wf/click?upn=3qP3xl6Iy86fMdiFlFopyBO5kQrx-2FesLRaGJ1fd7TCqcycBI512yYjvguVrkx1y-2Fz9KPysRoLmviHiSY-2BFOWSHOGao9s2cN5Lpg1UVge77rkWwwNcxZ2mVB26YZGOSbY_enNSH-2FxHAAqXgxiHRFihpX91d75ERAkpfg4XQfmYhCFb47pW8iDRAYLsM9sf9wIa92fG-2FsxU68zXzzMA2nO2VCunl2bm-2FhLXsWS8P3XITs28GUU7Mnlu37POPaWv9myv4YWBbFU9plZi0-2B42tZfBaUujH4mxA3e6jOiyDa5q6HKz45-2B17g6DUNL-2BnIMAR30MgNzZ9DkvwpKBFAc9iM2vRpBssSvozyZlo0kCIIx-2BXo94fBv-2Bzsiv6CWTwBWP6EgVmt-2Bc5-2BUjbiI9f9-2B3-2FKSmFR2sYKRgxSGS9xWt6wTEoPj-2F-2BcAAUiFsiSHRAVMO5ln71fNdX3yJ8OwDFeNCvyi9vE0VpqitGSUrdgHvdzZPGke2v0U3-2BctxMl3QEvgVuelRx5sCqhLzk4wXD1WNIyhE6xIHCz7kfAF6lVfNtz0tVpWLGFEebnlGG6lTylWKQAK2-2BP98PLFj9SQCBSkB9GEwTw-3D-3D) I know that together we can build a Republican Party in New Hampshire that is stronger than ever before - name: Jason Yates
subject: The shutdown reveals 1 thing
body:
| | Peter, If the government shutdown has highlighted anything, it’s the fact that our elected leaders _need_ our prayers. As Christians, we are called and commanded to pray for our leaders. **_I urge, then, first of all, that petitions, prayers, intercession, and thanksgiving be made for all people— for kings and all those in authority, that we may live peaceful and quiet lives in all godliness and holiness. (1 Timothy 2:1-2)_** Our team will be praying weekly for our elected leaders and our nation. I want to encourage you to join us. Take a few minutes to [pray today for your representatives and send them a note](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=tERu_zD2Ude52JyIiTcd_v3oLaYMt3-UiGv5-ziiNLs&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e190115p2a01&n=1) letting them know you are praying for them this session! [](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=tERu_zD2Ude52JyIiTcd_v3oLaYMt3-UiGv5-ziiNLs&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e190115p2a01&n=2) **United. We Stand.** Jason Yates
CEO

**To help share information and spread Governor Holcomb’s message, we are asking our supporters to use the #INSOTS and #NextLevel hashtags across social media while watching the address live.**

While you’ll have to tune in tonight to see Governor Holcomb’s full address, here is a preview of what to expect -- with his five governing pillars continuing to guide us into the new legislative session and taking Indiana to the Next Level:

_**Grow Indiana’s Economy**_
The foundation of a strong economy is fiscal responsibility, which Governor Holcomb is working to deliver through Indiana’s eighth consecutive balanced budget. By keeping our pensions funded, our debt low, and modernizing our tax code, we’re maintaining our status as the fiscal envy of the nation and attracting record levels of new high-wage Hoosier jobs to Indiana!

_**Maintain & Build the State's Infrastructure**_
Ask Governor Holcomb, and he’ll tell you that in the Crossroads of America, infrastructure is the key to connecting Hoosiers to each other and the world. Through our Next Level Connections initiative, announced in September, we will grow our infrastructure, including finishing I-69 three years early and expanding access to high-speed, affordable broadband to unserved communities.

_**Stand With Hoosiers Through Every Step of the Their Education**_
That includes early learning, high school, and workforce training as Hoosiers look to skill-up to fill today's new high-wage, high-demand jobs. Specifically, we’ll work to support Indiana schools and teachers with a funding boost, while giving schools flexibility to develop partnerships to expose students to more opportunities. We’ll also expand the Workforce Ready Grants and Employer Training Grants that are making Next Level Jobs such a big success.

_**Promote Public Health & Attack the Drug Epidemic**_
Working to help lift up Hoosier children and families is essential to promoting public health. We will adopt school safety recommendations, including more mental health tools and physical safety enhancements. This plan will help children in need through the Department of Child Services, as well as provide new tools to lower infant mortality and help those recovering from the drug epidemic.

_**Deliver Great Government Service**_
It’s time to modernize our state government technology, as well as streamline state boards and commissions to make it easier for Hoosiers to interact with their government.

_With these five pillars as guidance, 2019 will be a year of progress and concrete results for Hoosiers across the state, and Governor Holcomb is extremely excited to put this plan into action!_

**[Don’t forget to tune in TONIGHT at 7 p.m. ET/6 p.m. CT. Use this link to watch the livestream of Governor Holcomb’s 2019 State of the State Address.](http://links.holcombforindiana.com/u/click?_t=60bbc6d769fe4010b248f63c6cb0e872&_m=d3985cef3a5642b590b470e34848cd82&_e=5I5jft-gPZdMpKpCyVoZ10Gy99YSmVotMa5QbHKUwBaCr4Ns0ifcMRphtAZ5aH-0ElTmTnAGgKI28YWbduASOLF7i1ly5Wi1K7NkhN2TDIznZgh5VszdrOv_RRLS4JjmUJ__nZusXDdU2E_BKGWgis8OOPv-BPa35zvNkFicOjTX6XbdT3xxnXr6E6fBJADD3GfCYhKT4nzcmpP_19B8UiLC7r2kJJdIN5235pNAwy4lWhcokc-6ZG0paTsNyfccLq4wkvGMA3EElrBmI7rMnrfv_LBeKdpg6jBVq_Qk27hTghjQmlCEKgGk6NkRf-vCKaOe56TjymvLT8Eq6zunAIouRTiDpNhEqrcc4GavDESHz-_9V2ifpJ8Npgo81QuY_iAU2ulUMDRjeaThqBXOJA%3D%3D)**

| | Friends, We learned from our 2018 campaign that the power of the vote is often greater than the power of the message and cause. We also learned that unfortunately, not all voters have equal access to participate in the voting process. For students in college, just trying to vote can be difficult and discouraging. The same goes for our senior citizens who often vote by absentee ballots. Also, on the campaign trail, we learned that people were recently released from prison without knowing the status of their voting rights or the procedure to have them restored.
---

Not only that when combined, they make up a significant portion of the electorate, but they also share the brunt of consequences of most legislation that is debated and signed into law. If they can't or won't vote, their much needed voice is left out of the conversations lawmakers have in legislatures and on Capitol Hill.

clean_body: Friends, We learned from our 2018 campaign that the power of the vote is often greater than the power of the message and cause. We also lear | | | | | | | Our Right to Vote is at Stake! | | | | Friends, We learned from our 2018 campaign that the power of the vote is often greater than the power of the message and cause. We also learned that unfortunately, not all voters have equal access to participate in the voting process. For students in college, just trying to vote can be difficult and discouraging. The same goes for our senior citizens who often vote by absentee ballots. Also, on the campaign trail, we learned that people were recently released from prison without knowing the status of their voting rights or the procedure to have them restored. | | | | | | | What is so significant about these three demographics - senior citizens, college students, and the incarcerated? Not only that when combined, they make up a significant portion of the electorate, but they also share the brunt of consequences of most legislation that is debated and signed into law. If they can't or won't vote, their much needed voice is left out of the conversations lawmakers have in legislatures and on Capitol Hill. | | | | What Representative Anderson has done and plans to do for voting rights: On January 12, Rep. Anderson and other legislators from the Mississippi Future Caucus partnered with the Millennial Action Project to host the first biannual GENSummit on College Voting. The summit gave college students registered to vote in the State of Mississippi an opportunity to share stories and give input on their experiences with the voting process. This provided legislators the information needed to craft bills to change impractical voting laws. | | | | | | | | | | | | This week on the House Floor, Representative Anderson will be introducing legislation that will automatically restore the right to vote to all Mississippians who leave prison after serving their time. We hope you all will join us as we work together in the legislature and in our community to assure that everyone has a say in our upcoming elections. Onward,Friends For Jeramey | Share Via: | | |

name: Team Cooper
subject: Roy needs you to co-sign this letter to President Trump
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Cooper for North Carolina
body:
We're now in the longest federal government shutdown in history, and North Carolinians who are relying on federal aid to help rebuild from Hurricane Florence can't wait much longer. |
---
[]()
| | | Team -- We're now in the longest federal government shutdown in history, and North Carolinians who are relying on federal aid to help rebuild from Hurricane Florence can't wait much longer.

We need to keep up the pressure. Last week, Roy sent a letter to President Trump urging him to end his shutdown so North Carolina can continue to rebuild from hurricane damage and receive the federal support we urgently need. _**[Will you add your name as a co-signer to Roy's letter and urge President Trump to end his shutdown?](http://act.roycooper. "https://act.roycooper.com/page/s/co-sign-trump-end-shutdown-now?source=011519&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&city=Rocky Point&state=NC&zip=28457&phone=&occupation=&employer=")**_

Begin Forward from Roy Cooper:
* * *
Team,

Today, I sent a letter on behalf of all North Carolinians to President Trump urging him to end his government shutdown.

North Carolina continues to rebuild from hurricane flood damage and implement new changes to prevent future damage from natural disasters. When President Trump visited our state after Hurricane Florence, he promised 100% of the federal government's support. With his shutdown, that promise is nearly impossible to keep.

Our efforts to rebuild and strengthen North Carolina stall every day that federal funds are delayed.

[_**That 's why I'm coming to you and asking you to co-sign my letter to President Trump and urge him to end the shutdown now. Please, click here to add your name today.**_](http://act.roycooper. "https://act.roycooper.com/page/s/co-sign-trump-end-shutdown-now?source=011519&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&city=Rocky Point&state=NC&zip=28457&phone=&occupation=&employer=")

Without the support of the federal government to rebuild after hurricane damage, the future of our communities is at stake.

Our farmers can't get the help they need from the Department of Agriculture after many farms were devastated in the hurricane. And with planting season just around the corner, their livelihoods are at stake.

Entire cities, communities, and homes were destroyed by hurricane flood waters, yet without the support for HUD, we can't strengthen our infrastructure, protect property, and keep citizens out of harm's way of the next disaster.

The spirit of North Carolinians is strong, and our determination to rebuild better than ever has not wavered, but businesses, agriculture, and lives are at risk without the support from the federal government.

[_**With your help, we can send a powerful message to President Trump that the shutdown must end so we can rebuild. Add your name as a co-signer to my letter today.**_](http://act.roycooper. "https://act.roycooper.com/page/s/co-sign-trump-end-shutdown-now?source=011519&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&city=Rocky Point&state=NC&zip=28457&phone=&occupation=&employer=")

Thanks,

Roy
---

name: Todd Cheewing
subject: Republican State Committee Officer Election Registration
body:

American families are at risk. Migrant families are at risk. Children - on both sides of the border - are at risk.
